kind: structural: FFTW-style shared Bluestein plan across batched rows (bit-exact)
lever_summary: Hoist the per-row Bluestein chirp table + kernel-FFT into a BluesteinPlan built once per (n,direction) and reused across all rows of a batched FFT, with a reusable BluesteinScratch. Eliminates per-row ~3n sin_cos, the per-row kernel FFT, and 6 per-row allocations. Single calls also compute the chirp table once instead of 3 sin_cos loops.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed per-row Bluestein (pass95); normalized by eval/fft_256 (pow2 radix-2, unaffected) calibration.

change.lever: Added BluesteinPlan {chirp table, fb} + BluesteinScratch + apply_into; bluestein_dft_into builds a one-shot plan; eval_fft/eval_ifft build the plan once for non-pow2 n and reuse across rows. Added eval/fft_batch_128x1000 bench + complex_matrix helper.

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL to committed per-row Bluestein (pass95) — same chirp values, kernel FFT, products, inverse FFT, scaling; only hoisted/table-ized

validation.name: fft_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --release fft

validation.name: clippy
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: Builds on pass95 Bluestein. Headline win is the batched real-world case (2.17x). Next: rfft does a wasteful full-complex FFT — the half-size real-packing trick is a separate ~2x candidate.
